title: 7th victim dies after Easter N4 crash in Mpumalanga
description: NATIONAL NEWS - A seventh victim of...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - A seventh victim of Thursday’s N4 crash between Belfast and Machadodorp, near Berg-en-Dal in Mpumalanga, has died in hospital.

Middelburg Observer reports that the gruesome accident was one of two multiple-vehicle accidents just two kilometres apart.


> Traffic was backed up for kilometres towards Mbombela, with the road only opening at 18:00.


The accident took place at around 13:00 in dense fog.

Five people were declared dead on the scene, while a sixth passed away later in hospital on Thursday.

It was confirmed yesterday that a seventh victim of the accident passed away in Life Midmed Hospital.

The identities of the deceased have still not been released, with post-mortem examinations having taken place yesterday and today.What was left of one of the vehicles on the scene at Berg-en-Dal Thursday.


> Both Belfast and Middelburg mortuaries are in the process of finalising formal identification of the deceased.


Meanwhile, the provincial traffic department also said formal identification of all victims has not been finalised, and where identities have been confirmed, consultation with affected families is taking place.

Multiple vehicles were involved in the accident.

Most of the Easter road deaths in Mpumalanga happened in the Nkangala region.

Mpumalanga’s Easter road statistics will only be released once the national statistics have been published.

In general, road deaths seem to have declined over Easter in all provinces, apart from Mpumalanga, where they increased.


> Apart from the seven N4 fatalities after Thursday’s accident, another two people died when a taxi rolled on the N4 over the weekend.


A pedestrian was also struck and killed while crossing the highway.

Multiple other accidents also occurred, resulting in a further four deaths, three on the Bethal road and one outside Belfast on the Stoffberg road.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
